about_public_record.about_summary_narrative:
Brett F. Miller is an establishment-rooted Longview attorney with multi-generational ties to the community and the local high school. His public record is consistent with a lawyer-trustee archetype: Longview native, TCU and SMU Law educated, practicing at a locally prominent firm, active in the local federal bar, and serving on the school board during a period of significant district transitions including a new superintendent and a major bond-funded facilities program.

First elected in 2020 after challenging an incumbent educator, Miller was unopposed for reelection in 2023 and, absent a late filing surprise, is again on the ballot in 2026. His publicly stated motivations have been parental (three young children in LISD at the time of his first run) and civic, and he framed his 2020 candidacy as agenda-less curiosity about policy questions such as the SB 1882 charter conversion debate then before the district.

The expanded public-record dive on Miller is mostly open. Property records, voter roll history, firm entity filings, prior campaign finance reports, and his attorney-of-record history in federal court are all retrievable but not yet pulled. The single most important disclosure gap for voters is his legal practice: a practicing litigator can easily end up opposite LISD, LISD vendors, or other trustees in court, and voters should know if that has happened or is likely to.

No negative public-record items have surfaced as of this research date. The appropriate next step is the mechanical records pull described in the workflow, not speculation.

content_themes: Support for Longview ISD academic programs
Legal and governance perspective on district decisions
Enthusiasm for district leadership transitions (Supt. Sheppard hire)

notable_statements.quote_or_paraphrase: On first run, said he grew up in Longview, attended LISD, has three young children, and wanted a voice at the board table
Welcomed Dr. Marla Sheppard as superintendent, calling her credentials and enthusiasm a good fit for the district

education_policy_positions.bond_and_budget: Part of board majority that reduced M&O+I&S tax rate from $1.3276 (2022) to $1.1422 (2023-24); supportive of bond-funded facility upgrades
education_policy_positions.superintendent_views: Publicly supportive of Supt. Marla Sheppard hire in 2024
education_policy_positions.other: In 2020 stated interest in SB 1882 charter conversion debate

red_flags.description: Practicing litigator in same market as district; potential for firm matters to intersect with district vendors. No specific conflict identified as of 2026-04-16.

summary: Brett F. Miller is the incumbent Place 2 trustee on the Longview ISD Board. Longview native, third-generation LHS grad (2001), TCU undergrad, SMU Law. Practices family law, employment disputes, and business litigation at Miller Fair Henry PLLC. First elected 2020 after challenging Ava Welge, unopposed for reelection 2023. Second term ends May 2026 and Place 2 is on the 2026 ballot.
analyst_notes: Establishment-aligned, legally trained trustee. Longview roots, supportive of current superintendent, votes with board on bond and tax matters. No strongly partisan public footprint located in open sources as of research date. Priority next moves: pull campaign Facebook post history, confirm primary voting history via Gregg County voter rolls, check State Bar discipline, pull PACER attorney history, request LISD local campaign finance filings.
research_gaps: Confirm 2026 filing status with LISD elections coordinator Milagros Cordoba (903-381-2200)
Gregg County voter roll primary-history pull
PACER + Gregg County District Clerk civil / family / criminal record search
TEC + FEC donor-by-candidate search for donations given BY Miller
SOSDirect entity filings for Miller Fair Henry PLLC and related entities
LISD-posted campaign finance reports for 2020, 2023, and 2026 cycles
Wayback snapshots of firm bio and campaign Facebook
